description: CLI interface for PartnerStack Partner API

list.help: List rewards from the PartnerStack Partner API.

options.name: --filter
options.help: API filter, e.g. payment_status:eq:withdrawn or created_at:gte:1711034344078

options.name: --properties
options.help: Comma-separated fields to include, with dot-notation support

list.usage_instructions: Use `partnerstack rewards list` with the documented arguments and options shown below.

get.help: Get one reward by reward key.

options.name: --properties
options.help: Comma-separated fields to include, with dot-notation support

get.usage_instructions: Use `partnerstack rewards get` with the documented arguments and options shown below.

rewards.usage_instructions: Use `partnerstack rewards` to access this command group. Run `--help` on a subcommand for details.

list.help: List marketplace-listed programs (programs the partner has not necessarily joined).

options.name: --filter
options.help: API filter, e.g. category:eq:artificial-intelligence, created_at:gte:1711034344078, or created_at:lte:1748902440427

options.name: --properties
options.help: Comma-separated fields to include, with dot-notation support

list.usage_instructions: Use `partnerstack marketplace list` with the documented arguments and options shown below.

get.help: Retrieve a single marketplace program by company_key.

options.name: --properties
options.help: Comma-separated fields to include, with dot-notation support

get.usage_instructions: Use `partnerstack marketplace get` with the documented arguments and options shown below.

marketplace.usage_instructions: Use `partnerstack marketplace` to access this command group. Run `--help` on a subcommand for details.

form-templates.help: List PartnerStack form templates using Basic auth

list.help: List application form templates from GET /api/v2/form-templates.

options.name: --limit
options.help: RANGE [1<=x<=250]  Maximum form templates to return

options.name: --filter
options.help: API filter. Supported equality filters: group:eq:<slug>, target_type:eq:<value>, mold_keys:eq:<value>; created_at and updated_at support gte/lte with epoch-ms values

options.name: --properties
options.help: Comma-separated fields to include, with dot-notation support

list.usage_instructions: Use `partnerstack form-templates list` with the documented arguments and options shown below.

get.help: Get one form template by key.

arguments.name: form_template_key
arguments.help: Form template key (also exposed as 'id' on the model)

options.name: --properties
options.help: Comma-separated fields to include, with dot-notation support

get.usage_instructions: Use `partnerstack form-templates get` with the documented arguments and options shown below.

form-templates.usage_instructions: Use `partnerstack form-templates` to access this command group. Run `--help` on a subcommand for details.

applications.help: Create PartnerStack partner applications using Basic auth

create.help: Create a partner application against a target program.

options.name: --group-slug
options.help: Slug of the group to apply to. Discover valid slugs via 'partnerstack form-templates list' (the form-template's 'group' field). Documented examples: affiliates, resellers, referral, topsellers (vendor-defined)

options.name: --meta
options.help: Application meta object as a JSON string. Example: '{"first_name":"Jane","last_name":"Doe","email":"jane@example.com","business_name":"Example LLC"}'

options.name: --meta-field
options.help: Repeatable key=value pair appended to the meta object. Example: --meta-field first_name=Jane --meta-field email=jane@example.com

create.usage_instructions: Use `partnerstack applications create` with the documented arguments and options shown below.

applications.usage_instructions: Use `partnerstack applications` to access this command group. Run `--help` on a subcommand for details.

options.name: --filter
options.help: API filter. Supported: order_by:eq:<value>, has_sub_id:eq:<true|false|null>, include_offers:eq:<true|false>, include_archived:eq:<true|false>

options.name: --properties
options.help: Comma-separated fields to include, with dot-notation support

list.usage_instructions: Use `partnerstack partnerships list` with the documented arguments and options shown below.

get.help: Get one partnership by key. PartnerStack does not document a `GET /partnerships/{key}` endpoint; this command pages through `GET /api/v2/partnerships` (the documented partner endpoint) and returns the partnership whose `key` matches.

arguments.name: partnership_key
arguments.help: Partnership key (or value of the model's `id`)

options.name: --properties
options.help: Comma-separated fields to include, with dot-notation support

get.usage_instructions: Use `partnerstack partnerships get` with the documented arguments and options shown below.

partnerships.usage_instructions: Use `partnerstack partnerships` to access this command group. Run `--help` on a subcommand for details.

login.help: Configure authentication credentials. Prompts for required credentials based on the tool's authentication type. For OAuth authorization code flows, opens a browser for user consent.

options.name: --profile
options.help: Profile name to save credentials to

login.usage_instructions: Use `partnerstack auth login` with the documented arguments and options shown below.

options.name: --profile
options.help: Profile name to clear credentials from

logout.usage_instructions: Use `partnerstack auth logout` with the documented arguments and options shown below.

status.help: Check authentication status across profiles. Performs a live round-trip for every configured credential type so the report reflects ground truth — not on-disk belief. Saved credentials whose live verification fails are reported as ``authenticated: false`` with the failure reason in ``api_test``.

options.name: --profile
options.help: Profile name to check (defaults to all profiles)

status.usage_instructions: Use `partnerstack auth status` with the documented arguments and options shown below.

test.help: Test authentication by verifying credentials work across profiles.
test.usage_instructions: Use `partnerstack auth test` with the documented arguments and options shown below.

login-basic.help: Configure PartnerStack Basic auth public/secret keys.

options.name: --profile
options.help: Profile name to save Basic credentials to

login-basic.usage_instructions: Use `partnerstack auth login-basic` with the documented arguments and options shown below.

basic-status.help: Show whether PartnerStack Basic auth credentials are configured.
basic-status.usage_instructions: Use `partnerstack auth basic-status` with the documented arguments and options shown below.

list.help: List all profiles and show which is the default.

options.name: --limit
options.help: Maximum number of profiles to return

options.name: --properties
options.help: Comma-separated list of fields to include

list.usage_instructions: Use `partnerstack auth profiles list` with the documented arguments and options shown below.

get.help: Get details for a specific profile.

arguments.name: name
arguments.help: Profile name to get details for

get.usage_instructions: Use `partnerstack auth profiles get` with the documented arguments and options shown below.

create.help: Create a new profile from .env.example template.
create.usage_instructions: Use `partnerstack auth profiles create` with the documented arguments and options shown below.

set-default.help: Set a profile as the default (IS_DEFAULT_PROFILE=1).

arguments.name: name
arguments.help: Profile name to set as default

set-default.usage_instructions: Use `partnerstack auth profiles set-default` with the documented arguments and options shown below.

delete.help: Delete a profile and its data.
delete.usage_instructions: Use `partnerstack auth profiles delete` with the documented arguments and options shown below.

profiles.usage_instructions: Use `partnerstack auth profiles` to access this command group. Run `--help` on a subcommand for details.

auth.usage_instructions: Use `partnerstack auth` to access this command group. Run `--help` on a subcommand for details.

clear.help: Remove all cached responses. Deletes every file in the cache directory ({storage_dir}/cache/) and reports how many files and bytes were freed.
clear.usage_instructions: Use `partnerstack cache clear` with the documented arguments and options shown below.

cache.usage_instructions: Use `partnerstack cache` to access this command group. Run `--help` on a subcommand for details.

usage_instructions: Use `` with the documented arguments and options shown below.
